Skip to contents
mermaidrcovariates 1.0.0
  • Reference
    • Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations
  • Changelog

Getting environmental covariates for GFCR locations

covariates.Rmd

This analysis extracts covariates for GFCR project sites from MERMAID, then combines that with coral cover data to plot the relationship between the two. In this example, the environmental data is maximum daily Sea Surface Temperature (SST) for a given number of days prior to the survey.

Load packages

Load libraries to use for analysis.

# remotes::install_github("data-mermaid/mermaidr")
# remotes::install_github("data-mermaid/mermaidr-covariates")

library(mermaidr)
library(mermaidrcovariates)
library(tidyverse)

Get project data from MERMAID

The first step is to get coral cover data for GFCR projects from MERMAID. The following gets summary sample events, and then filters for projects whose tags contain “GFCR”, and projects that have hard coral cover data.

summary_sampleevents <- mermaid_get_summary_sampleevents()

gfcr_summary_sampleevents <- summary_sampleevents %>%
  filter(str_detect(tags, "GFCR")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(`benthicpit_percent_cover_benthic_category_avg_Hard coral`)) %>%
  rename(hard_coral_cover = `benthicpit_percent_cover_benthic_category_avg_Hard coral`)

gfcr_summary_sampleevents
## # A tibble: 142 × 385
##    project_id project tags  country site_id site  latitude longitude reef_type reef_zone
##    <chr>      <chr>   <chr> <chr>   <chr>   <chr>    <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>     <chr>    
##  1 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef
##  2 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef
##  3 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef
##  4 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef
##  5 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef
##  6 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef
##  7 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef
##  8 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef
##  9 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  399b38… Aqab…     29.4      35.0 fringing  fore reef
## 10 1370aaba-… Gulf o… Glob… Jordan  04e931… Nort…     29.5      35.0 fringing  fore reef
## # ℹ 132 more rows
## # ℹ 375 more variables: reef_exposure <chr>, management_id <chr>, management <chr>,
## #   management_est_year <int>, management_size <dbl>, management_parties <chr>,
## #   management_compliance <chr>, management_rules <chr>, sample_date <date>,
## #   data_policy_beltfish <chr>, data_policy_benthiclit <chr>,
## #   data_policy_benthicpit <chr>, data_policy_benthicpqt <chr>,
## #   data_policy_habitatcomplexity <chr>, data_policy_bleachingqc <chr>, …

Summary of hard coral cover for projects

Summarise projects to show their tags, country, number of sites, and average hard coral cover.

gfcr_summary_sampleevents %>%
  group_by(project, tags, country) %>%
  summarise(
    n_sites = length(site),
    average_hard_coral_cover = mean(hard_coral_cover), .groups = "drop"
  )
## # A tibble: 7 × 5
##   project                                   tags  country n_sites average_hard_coral_c…¹
##   <chr>                                     <chr> <chr>     <int>                  <dbl>
## 1 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme   Glob… Jordan       29                  21.4 
## 2 Investing in Coral Reefs and the Blue Ec… Unit… Fiji         53                  30.9 
## 3 Maldives RREEF (GFCR)                     Glob… Maldiv…      11                  43.4 
## 4 SLCRI Bar Reef Seascape                   Inte… Sri La…      11                   9.68
## 5 SLCRI Kayankerni Seascape                 Inte… Sri La…      11                  44.4 
## 6 SLCRI Pigeon Island Seascape              Inte… Sri La…       7                  55.9 
## 7 Terumbu Karang Sehat Indonesia Program -… Cons… Indone…      20                  36.9 
## # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​average_hard_coral_cover

Get covariates for these sites

Next, get the relevant covariates for these sites.

List covariates

List available covariates.

list_covariates()
## # A tibble: 17 × 11
##    title          description start_date end_date   license `sci:doi` keywords providers
##    <chr>          <chr>       <date>     <date>     <chr>   <chr>     <chr>    <list>   
##  1 50 Reefs+ pri… This datas… 2026-02-22 2026-02-22 CC-BY-… 10.5281/… conserv… <tibble> 
##  2 ACA Benthic H… The Alan C… 2018-01-01 2021-01-01 propri… <NA>      ACA, Al… <tibble> 
##  3 ACA Reef Exte… ACA reef e… 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      reef, r… <tibble> 
##  4 Country Bound… This datas… 2024-10-04 2024-10-04 other   10.14284… adminis… <tibble> 
##  5 Daily Sea Sur… Sea surfac… 1985-01-01 2026-01-13 CC0-1.0 <NA>      climate… <tibble> 
##  6 Human populat… Coastal po… 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coastal… <tibble> 
##  7 Marine Ecoreg… A biogeogr… 2011-11-18 2011-11-18 CC-BY-… 10.1641/… biodive… <tibble> 
##  8 Market gravit… From econo… 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coral r… <tibble> 
##  9 Number of por… Port locat… 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coral r… <tibble> 
## 10 Tourism index  Intensive … 2021-12-28 2021-12-28 MIT Li… 10.1111/… coral r… <tibble> 
## 11 GPW Country B… This datas… 2024-10-04 2024-10-04 other   10.14284… adminis… <tibble> 
## 12 GPW Dispersal… Dispersal … 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      dispers… <tibble> 
## 13 GPW Global Se… Global Sed… 2000-01-01 2020-01-01 propri… <NA>      exposur… <tibble> 
## 14 GPW Global Se… Global sed… 2000-01-01 2020-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      gpw, se… <tibble> 
## 15 GPW Land Use … Land Use a… 2000-01-01 2020-01-01 CC0-1.0 <NA>      land co… <tibble> 
## 16 GPW MEOW Real… Marine Eco… 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 cc-by-… <NA>      biodive… <tibble> 
## 17 GPW Watersheds Watersheds… 2026-01-01 2026-01-01 CC0 1.… <NA>      gpw, hy… <tibble> 
## # ℹ 3 more variables: `sci:citation` <chr>, bbox <list>, id <chr>

Get maximum SST for previous year

Focus on Sea Surface Temperature, and get the max for the 30 days prior to the survey data. Review the survey data:

gfcr_summary_sampleevents %>%
  distinct(project, site, latitude, longitude, sample_date)
## # A tibble: 142 × 5
##    project                                 site           latitude longitude sample_date
##    <chr>                                   <chr>             <dbl>     <dbl> <date>     
##  1 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2024-12-06 
##  2 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2024-12-01 
##  3 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2025-10-28 
##  4 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2024-12-09 
##  5 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2025-07-30 
##  6 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2025-10-29 
##  7 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2025-09-01 
##  8 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2024-12-08 
##  9 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Aqaba Marine …     29.4      35.0 2024-12-24 
## 10 Gulf of Aqaba Resilient Reefs Programme Northern Deep…     29.5      35.0 2025-10-27 
## # ℹ 132 more rows

We can access daily SST data by using the function get_zonal_statistics(), which takes the site latitude and longitude, as well as the survey date, to find the data at that site for n_days days prior, within the given radius, and spatially aggregates it according to the specified spatial_stat.

For example, to get the daily SST for each of the 30 days prior to (and including) the sample event, using the mean SST within 100m of the sites:

daily_sst <- gfcr_summary_sampleevents %>%
  get_zonal_statistics("Daily Sea Surface Temperature", n_days = 30, radius = 100, spatial_stats = "mean")

Look at the returned data, keeping only the project information, site, survey date, hard coral cover, and zonal_statistics

daily_sst <- daily_sst %>%
  select(site, sample_date, hard_coral_cover, zonal_statistics)

daily_sst
## # A tibble: 142 × 4
##    site                          sample_date hard_coral_cover zonal_statistics 
##    <chr>                         <date>                 <dbl> <list>           
##  1 Sand Island Leeward           2023-05-24              38.5 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  2 Sand Island South East        2023-05-24              38.2 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  3 Elephant point East           2023-05-25              25.6 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  4 Elephant point West           2023-05-25              15   <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  5 Sand Island North East        2023-05-25              63   <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  6 Pasikudah nearshore           2023-05-28              45.5 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  7 Pasikudah offshore            2023-05-28              22.5 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  8 Kalkudah                      2023-05-29              46   <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  9 Pirate cove North to Headland 2023-06-14              73.5 <tibble [30 × 5]>
## 10 Pigeon island Landward - 01   2023-06-16              73.6 <tibble [30 × 5]>
## # ℹ 132 more rows

Expand zonal statistics

The zonal statistics are returned in a format that need to be expanded. Once they are, you can see they contain daily mean SST values:

daily_sst %>%
  unnest(zonal_statistics)
## # A tibble: 3,738 × 8
##    site                sample_date hard_coral_cover covariate                    
##    <chr>               <date>                 <dbl> <chr>                        
##  1 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature
##  2 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature
##  3 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature
##  4 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature
##  5 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature
##  6 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature
##  7 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature
##  8 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature
##  9 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature
## 10 Sand Island Leeward 2023-05-24              38.5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature
##    date        band spatial_stat value
##    <date>     <dbl> <chr>        <dbl>
##  1 2023-05-24     1 mean          30.6
##  2 2023-05-23     1 mean          30.5
##  3 2023-05-22     1 mean          30.5
##  4 2023-05-21     1 mean          30.5
##  5 2023-05-20     1 mean          30.5
##  6 2023-05-19     1 mean          30.4
##  7 2023-05-18     1 mean          30.1
##  8 2023-05-17     1 mean          30.2
##  9 2023-05-16     1 mean          30.0
## 10 2023-05-15     1 mean          30.0
## # ℹ 3,728 more rows

Summarise zonal statistics

To get the maximum SST over the 30 day period, we use summarise_zonal_statistics(), specifying that the temporal statistic is the max:

max_sst <- daily_sst %>%
  summarise_zonal_statistics("max")

max_sst
## # A tibble: 142 × 5
##    site                          sample_date hard_coral_cover zonal_statistics 
##    <chr>                         <date>                 <dbl> <list>           
##  1 Sand Island Leeward           2023-05-24              38.5 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  2 Sand Island South East        2023-05-24              38.2 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  3 Elephant point East           2023-05-25              25.6 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  4 Elephant point West           2023-05-25              15   <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  5 Sand Island North East        2023-05-25              63   <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  6 Pasikudah nearshore           2023-05-28              45.5 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  7 Pasikudah offshore            2023-05-28              22.5 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  8 Kalkudah                      2023-05-29              46   <tibble [30 × 5]>
##  9 Pirate cove North to Headland 2023-06-14              73.5 <tibble [30 × 5]>
## 10 Pigeon island Landward - 01   2023-06-16              73.6 <tibble [30 × 5]>
##    summary_zonal_statistics
##    <list>                  
##  1 <tibble [1 × 8]>        
##  2 <tibble [1 × 8]>        
##  3 <tibble [1 × 8]>        
##  4 <tibble [1 × 8]>        
##  5 <tibble [1 × 8]>        
##  6 <tibble [1 × 8]>        
##  7 <tibble [1 × 8]>        
##  8 <tibble [1 × 8]>        
##  9 <tibble [1 × 8]>        
## 10 <tibble [1 × 8]>        
## # ℹ 132 more rows

The original zonal statistics are returned along with the summarised zonal statistics, which we expand. Now, we only have one value for each sample event – the maximum SST. This is returned along with the start and end date of the covariate data used, as well as the number of dates. This is useful information cases where the covariate data may not be available for all requested dates.

max_sst <- max_sst %>%
  select(-zonal_statistics) %>%
  unnest(summary_zonal_statistics)

max_sst
## # A tibble: 142 × 11
##    site                          sample_date hard_coral_cover
##    <chr>                         <date>                 <dbl>
##  1 Sand Island Leeward           2023-05-24              38.5
##  2 Sand Island South East        2023-05-24              38.2
##  3 Elephant point East           2023-05-25              25.6
##  4 Elephant point West           2023-05-25              15  
##  5 Sand Island North East        2023-05-25              63  
##  6 Pasikudah nearshore           2023-05-28              45.5
##  7 Pasikudah offshore            2023-05-28              22.5
##  8 Kalkudah                      2023-05-29              46  
##  9 Pirate cove North to Headland 2023-06-14              73.5
## 10 Pigeon island Landward - 01   2023-06-16              73.6
##    covariate                     start_date end_date   n_dates  band spatial_stat
##    <chr>                         <date>     <date>       <dbl> <dbl> <chr>       
##  1 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-25 2023-05-24      30     1 mean        
##  2 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-25 2023-05-24      30     1 mean        
##  3 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-26 2023-05-25      30     1 mean        
##  4 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-26 2023-05-25      30     1 mean        
##  5 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-26 2023-05-25      30     1 mean        
##  6 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-29 2023-05-28      30     1 mean        
##  7 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-29 2023-05-28      30     1 mean        
##  8 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-04-30 2023-05-29      30     1 mean        
##  9 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-05-16 2023-06-14      30     1 mean        
## 10 Daily Sea Surface Temperature 2023-05-18 2023-06-16      30     1 mean        
##    temporal_stat value
##    <chr>         <dbl>
##  1 max            30.6
##  2 max            30.6
##  3 max            30.6
##  4 max            30.6
##  5 max            30.6
##  6 max            30.7
##  7 max            30.7
##  8 max            30.7
##  9 max            30.2
## 10 max            30.3
## # ℹ 132 more rows

Visualize

Finally, visualize coral cover against the maximum Sea Surface Temperature for the past 30 days.

ggplot(
  max_sst,
  aes(
    x = value,
    y = hard_coral_cover
  )
) +
  geom_point(color = "darkblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = paste("Coral Cover vs. Max. SST (Previous 30 Days)"),
    x = "Maximum SST",
    y = "Hard Coral Cover (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

On this page

Developed by Sharla Gelfand, Wildlife Conservation Society.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
